key_insight: The paper shows how to compute stress accurately in localized-basis DFT, including linear-scaling formulations, enabling reliable pressure-dependent simulations at large scale.
engineering_relevance: This enables stable constant-pressure molecular dynamics and scalable materials modeling in the Conquest code for systems too large for conventional cubic-scaling DFT.
method: Derive stress expressions for DFT with a localized orbital basis in both exact-diagonalization and linear-scaling modes.
Evaluate stress convergence versus density-matrix range cutoff across materials with different band gaps.
Demonstrate implementation and test stability in large-scale isothermal-isobaric molecular dynamics.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that stress converges well with modest density-matrix cutoffs and that large-scale NPT molecular dynamics remains stable and accurate.
watch_item: Accuracy depends on choosing an adequate density-matrix range cutoff, so poorly converged truncation can affect stress and pressure control.
paper_title: Stress calculation in linear scaling DFT: convergence and dynamics
